domain: Tlingit clan crest totem pole composite reading (bottom figure most important; composite reading depends on paired clan-story knowledge; same crest means different things in different clan ownership contexts; master storyteller points sequentially to figures while reciting)
specific_mechanism: Composite artifact decomposed into K stacked figures; reading order is bottom-up; each figure requires anchor lookup into clan-specific story corpus; meaning is context-disambiguated by ownership.
llm_application: TOTEM-DECODE: K-segment decoder that generates the deepest/most-important segment first, then builds upward; each segment performs a grounded retrieval against a shared corpus (clan-stories analog = task-knowledge-base) to disambiguate context-dependent tokens; the same surface form is intentionally allowed to resolve to different segments based on a per-segment 'ownership' (clan-context) tag inherited from upstream segments. Bottom-up reverse order + per-segment context-disambiguation grounding is the kernel.
novelty_claim: Standard autoregressive decoders generate left-to-right. Tree-of-Thoughts, ReAct, and Reflexion compose intermediate reasoning steps but go forward in time. NEXUSSUM hierarchically summarizes; PECAN uses DAG-of-Information-Points; PipeSpec hierarchical speculation across models. TOTEM-DECODE differs by EXPLICIT BOTTOM-UP segment-recitation ORDER (deepest first) + PER-SEGMENT CLAN-CONTEXT DISAMBIGUATION TAG that allows surface-form polysemy resolution by inherited ownership context.
